name: Jordan Pack
signatureMoves: TOUR OF THE WOODS (tour of the islands)
whyImHere:
Jordan Pack did not come to OWL for fame, championships, or acceptance. Known only as “The Mad Wolf,” Pack emerged from the woods of Durango, Colorado, where rumors claim he was raised among wolves. Whether those stories are true remains unknown—and Pack has never cared enough to explain himself.

A loner by nature, he enters the ring wearing a wolf headdress and a straitjacket, unleashing years of frustration through overwhelming strength and brutal violence. Pack displays the instincts of a predator: patient, territorial, relentless, and always ready to strike. Wrestling gives him a place to release the rage that has followed him out of the wilderness. He is not here to belong. He is here to hunt.

name: Errol Capleton
whyImHere:
Errol Capleton carries the rhythm, pride, and fighting spirit of Jamaica everywhere he goes. Known as “Bad Mon” and celebrated by many as the Pride of Jamaica, Capleton enters OWL determined to represent his homeland on the biggest stage possible. His energy is infectious, but opponents quickly learn that the smile disappears once the bell rings.

Capleton relies on explosive speed, sharp instincts, and unpredictable strikes that can end a match before his opponent realizes they are in danger. One moment he is dancing just outside their reach; the next, they are staring up at the lights. He fights with confidence, heart, and the belief that every victory belongs not only to him, but to everyone back home cheering his name.

name: Killian Crowe
whyImHere:
Killian Crowe did not come to OWL seeking attention. Attention inevitably finds him. Operating from the shadows, Crowe studies every opponent, every weakness, and every careless decision before choosing the perfect moment to strike. Nothing he does is accidental; every movement carries purpose, and every victory is another piece of a design only he fully understands.

Crowe refuses to be defined as hero, villain, or anything so ordinary. He considers himself an enigma—an independent force bound to no cause and controlled by no one. OWL offers stronger competition, greater influence, and a larger stage upon which to expose those who mistake mystery for weakness. By the time his opponents understand Killian Crowe, it is already far too late.

name: Nick Stone
whyImHere:
Nick Stone earned the name “Hard to Kill” the hard way. Across the independent circuit, he built a reputation for surviving punishment that would finish anyone else—and then finding another gear when the match mattered most. Stone brings rare flair to the ring, blending speed, power, and showmanship into a style that is impossible to ignore.

He is flashy because he has earned the right to be. Every pose, taunt, and confident grin is backed by the ability to deliver once the bell rings. Stone does not confuse confidence with arrogance; he simply knows exactly how good he is. After becoming a favorite everywhere he competed, he came to OWL to prove that his success was never limited to the independents. Bigger stage, tougher opponents—same result.

name: Ronin Fairchild
whyImHere:
Ronin Fairchild arrived in OWL with the confidence of a man who has never been told no—and the pedigree to make sure no one forgets it. Hailing from Chelsea and raised in wealth, privilege, and refinement, Fairchild presents himself as the perfect gentleman: polished, educated, and convinced he is the standard everyone else should be judged against.

But beneath the manners and sophistication is a ruthless competitor who will bend the rules whenever it serves him. Like any true aristocrat, Fairchild believes winning is simply his birthright. Backed by elite training money could buy and a smug certainty that he is physically and mentally superior, he steps into OWL to prove that class, skill, and status still reign above all. In Ronin Fairchild’s world, excellence should always look expensive.

name: Mack Aleven
whyImHere:
Mack Aleven knows what it means to come from a place where people expect your story to end before it ever really begins. Raised in the hard streets of southwest Atlanta, “Bulletproof” once lived with the kind of energy, confidence, and street mentality that made him respected—but also nearly cost him everything. After catching a gun charge, Aleven turned to wrestling as his way out, trading the streets for the ring without losing the swagger that made him who he is.

At just 5'6", he gets overlooked until the bell rings. Then the underestimation disappears fast. Mack moves with explosive agility, fearless instincts, and the sharp confidence of a young street general who refuses to back down. He is here to prove that survival made him dangerous—but wrestling made him unstoppable.

name: Riley King
whyImHere:
Riley King carries himself like a man who already knows where he belongs—at the top. Known as “The Golden Child,” King believes his years on the independent scene have prepared him to stand among the best young wrestlers in the industry. He is not chasing hype or living off potential. He sees himself as the next great standard-bearer, built on skill, confidence, and a bloodline of wrestling excellence reflected in his kingly identity.

In the ring, King combines sharp technique, quick instincts, and the kind of polished all-around ability that makes him dangerous against anyone. But for all the talent and pedigree, his purpose is simple: win. Riley King came to OWL for one thing and one thing only—gold. If greatness runs in his lineage, then championships are his destiny.

name: Jayden Alfah
whyImHere:
Jayden Alfah grew up seeing wrestling and anime the same way: as stories about people refusing to stay down when the world tells them they have reached their limit. Whenever life knocked him off course, he treated it like another chapter—not the ending. That mindset followed him into wrestling, where every setback became training, every rival became a new obstacle, and every match became part of his climb toward something greater.

Inspired by the intensity and imagination of his favorite wrestler, Kenny Omega, Jayden developed a style built around explosive offense, dramatic momentum shifts, and relentless resolve. He calls himself “Omega Level” because he believes there is always another transformation waiting inside him. Jayden came to OWL to prove that main-character determination is not fantasy—it is what happens when someone refuses to quit before reaching the final chapter.

name: Allen Rain
whyImHere:
Rain Allen was born in Toronto, but everything about him feels like a warning from the sky before disaster hits. Known as “The Storm Bringer,” Allen carries himself with quiet focus until the bell rings—then the calm disappears. His style is sudden, sharp, and unpredictable, built on fast strikes, explosive movement, and the ability to change the pace of a match without warning.

Rain does not need to shout to make people feel his presence. He believes pressure reveals the truth: who can survive, who will break, and who was never ready for the storm in the first place. OWL gives him the perfect place to test that theory. Every opponent steps into the forecast. Rain Allen decides how bad the damage gets.

name: Fabian Atlas
whyImHere:
Fabian Atlas does not enter OWL like an ordinary wrestler. He arrives like a monument brought to life. Known as “The Greek Physique,” Atlas has been called a man chiseled from marble, built with the strength, posture, and presence of something out of myth. But beneath the godlike image is a competitor who believes power should be earned, controlled, and used with purpose.

Atlas carries himself with pride, not arrogance. He sees the ring as his arena, the fans as his city, and every opponent as another trial placed before him. His strength is not just for show; he uses it to overwhelm, ground, and subdue anyone reckless enough to test him. Fabian Atlas came to OWL to prove that legends are not only remembered from the past. Some are still being built.

name: Percy Franklin
whyImHere:
Percy Franklin did not come up through bright lights and clean opportunities. He was built in back rooms, basement fights, late-night deals, and places where respect had to be taken before it was ever given. Known as “The Underground Boss,” Franklin carries himself like a man who already controls the room before he walks into it.

He is smooth, calculated, and dangerous—equal parts street general and fight promoter, the kind of man who understands that power is not always loud. Sometimes it is quiet, patient, and waiting for the right moment to collect. Percy sees OWL as a new territory, and every opponent as someone standing in the way of expansion. He is not here to play wrestler. He is here to build an empire, one body at a time.

name: Kwame Mensah
whyImHere:
Kwame Mensah was never meant to be the face of OWL’s future. He was supposed to be insurance. When whispers spread that a major signing could change the balance of power, someone behind the scenes went searching for a weapon they believed they could influence, control, and point in the right direction.

What they found in Accra, Ghana, was something far more dangerous.

Known as “The Gold Coast King,” Mensah is a giant with conquest in his blood and world domination on his mind. He does not see OWL as a workplace. He sees it as territory waiting to be claimed. The people who brought him in may think they hold the leash, but Kwame believes kings answer to no one. Now that he is here, OWL has to find out whether it signed a protector—or unleashed a tyrant.

name: Paddy Saints
whyImHere:
Paddy Saints earned the name “Bad Luck” long before he ever stepped into OWL. Back in Ireland, he was known as the thug for the pub—the man you called when talking was finished and someone needed to be dealt with. Street fights made his reputation, and by those standards, Paddy was damn near unbeatable.

Now he has come to the States to rep the green, make some green, and follow the hard-hitting path carved by men like Sheamus and Fit Finlay. The American wrestling style has not come easy, and Paddy is still learning how to turn chaos into clean victories. But win or lose, one thing never changes: if you run into Paddy Saints, your night is going to end badly. That is why they call him Bad Luck.

name: Travis Cudi
whyImHere:
Travis Cudi does not come to OWL polished, packaged, or pretending to be anything he is not. He comes straight out of Texas with crashout energy, a mean streak, and a reputation built on making fights ugly fast. Known as “The Lone Star Killer” and “Mr. Belt 2 Ass,” Cudi walks into every match like somebody owes him money, respect, or both.

He is not here to wrestle pretty. He is here to bring Texas violence to anyone standing across from him. Cudi has already made noise on the independent scene, including being promoted as the MPX Prospect Champion and appearing for Texas-based events under that same Lone Star Killer identity.

OWL gives him a bigger stage, but the mission stays simple: talk less, swing first, and leave people remembering exactly what happens when Travis Cudi takes off the belt.

name: Atua Tupu
whyImHere:
Every wrestling promotion needs a Samoan force. OWL did not just get one—they got a damn god.

Atua Tupu arrived carrying the weight of island pride, ancestral power, and a presence that feels bigger than the ring itself. Known as “The Samoan God,” Tupu wrestles like a storm given human form: heavy hands, crushing power, and a calm brutality that makes every movement feel inevitable. He does not rush. He does not panic. He simply advances, and opponents either move or get broken.

But Atua is not just here to be feared. He believes his bloodline carries a responsibility to dominate, protect legacy, and prove that greatness is not claimed through words—it is carved into people. OWL wanted a Samoan. What it received was something closer to a prophecy.

name: Chris Danger
whyImHere:
Chris Danger is proof that sometimes a created wrestler becomes bigger than the screen that introduced him. What started as a custom character on DenkOps’ WWE 2K MyCareer series turned into a full-blown wrestling identity, followed by fans who watched Danger go from digital underdog to real-life competitor. The character first broke through during WWE 2K15 content, eventually becoming a staple of DenkOps’ wrestling universe.

But Danger did not stay fictional. Chris Denker trained for the ring, debuted in 2021, and brought the name to life in front of real crowds. OWL gives him another chance to blur that line between fantasy and reality. He is here to prove this was never just a gimmick, a game, or an internet story. Chris Danger is here because the fans created the legend—but now he has to prove the legend can survive.

name: Kai Valentino
whyImHere:
Kai Valentino was never interested in blending in. Born and raised in Miami, he learned early that confidence could be armor, identity could be power, and attention was only dangerous if you did not know what to do with it. As OWL’s first openly gay wrestler, Kai does not carry that history quietly. He wears it with pride, style, and the kind of presence that makes every entrance feel like an event.

Known as “The Main Attraction,” Valentino believes wrestling needs more than toughness. It needs charisma, courage, and someone bold enough to make the spotlight follow them. Miami gave him flash, rhythm, and attitude, but the ring gave him purpose. Kai came to OWL to prove he is not here as a symbol or a side story. He is here to win, shine, and make sure nobody looks away.

name: Mascara Mota
whyImHere:
Mascara Mota was born in Tijuana, where the lucha libre tradition is loud, fast, colorful, and impossible to fake. Known as “El Lechuga Diablo,” Mota built his name by mixing classic high-flying lucha with a laid-back, unpredictable personality that keeps opponents guessing. He may walk in smiling, vibing, and acting like nothing bothers him, but once the bell rings, he becomes a blur.

They call him “The HIIIIIGH Flyin’” for a reason. He believes wrestling should feel like a party, a fight, and a magic trick all at once. Inspired by Tijuana’s wild energy and his own cannabis-fueled creativity, he turns every match into controlled chaos. His 420 Splash does not just end matches—it leaves opponents wondering what the hell just landed on them. Mascara Mota came to OWL to elevate lucha, elevate himself, and stay higher than the competition.

name: Cameron "Cam" Flash
whyImHere:
Cameron “Cam” Flash talked himself into OWL before he ever wrestled his way in. Known online as Mr. 4K, he built his name as an annoying streamer, loudmouth wrestling critic, and proud member of the internet wrestling community—the kind of guy who watched one match and immediately claimed he could do it better. After one dare too many, Cam stepped into a ring to prove the internet right. The problem? The internet may have lied.

Flash is flashy, obnoxious, and convinced every match is content. His nickname sounds elite, but “4K” is also the follower count he cannot seem to break past. Most of his offense is questionable at best: mistimed dives, ugly landings, and moves that only work when gravity feels generous. But every now and then, Cam catches someone slipping and turns a botch into an opportunity. He may not be good yet—but he is loud enough to make OWL notice.

name: Ren Takeda
whyImHere:
Ren Takeda was born in Tokyo, but his legend was shaped in the quiet hours after the crowds went home. While others chased fame, Takeda trained in silence, obsessed with becoming something rare: a wrestler with the patience of a predator, the precision of a fighter, and the spirit of a warrior who never wastes motion.

Known as “The Golden Tiger,” Takeda carries himself with calm intensity until the bell rings. Then the stillness disappears, replaced by sudden strikes, explosive movement, and a killer instinct hidden behind his composed expression. The tiger is not loud because it does not need to be. It waits, watches, and attacks when the opening appears.

Takeda came to OWL to prove that greatness does not always roar. Sometimes it stalks quietly from Tokyo, wrapped in gold, until the whole world realizes it is already too late.

name: Lio Rush
whyImHere:
Lio Rush has always been faster than the room could understand, but Blackheart is something different entirely. The man once known for flash, confidence, and impossible speed has evolved into something darker—less interested in attention and more interested in control. Every twitch, crawl, strike, and sudden burst of offense feels designed to make opponents uncomfortable before they ever get hurt.

As ROH World TV Champion, Rush brings championship credibility with a mind that no longer operates by normal rules. He is still explosive, still brilliant, still capable of ending a match in a blink—but now there is something haunting behind the eyes. Blackheart Lio Rush came to OWL because chaos travels well, fear spreads fast, and no one is ever truly ready for what he has become.

name: Ilja Dragunov
whyImHere:
Ilja Dragunov does not wrestle like a man trying to win. He wrestles like a man trying to survive something only he can see. Known as “The Mad Dragon” and Unbesiegbar, Dragunov built his reputation on violence, endurance, and an almost uncomfortable willingness to absorb punishment just to give it back harder. His wars across NXT UK, NXT, and WWE proved that pain is not something he avoids—it is something he weaponizes. He is a former NXT UK Champion and NXT Champion, with a career defined by intensity and championship-level credibility.

In OWL, Dragunov is not here for theatrics or shortcuts. He is here to test the limits of every opponent placed in front of him. Every chop, suplex, headbutt, and Torpedo Moscow carries the same message: Ilja Dragunov can be hurt, but he cannot be broken.

name: Kyle Fletcher
whyImHere:
Kyle Fletcher is no longer just the talented young half of a great tag team. He is “The Protostar,” a future star that stopped waiting for permission to explode. In AEW, Fletcher’s transformation through the Don Callis Family turned him colder, sharper, and far more dangerous, choosing ambition over friendship and proving he was willing to betray even close allies to climb higher. AEW has presented him as a major Callis Family weapon, including his pursuit of the AEW International Championship against former ally Konosuke Takeshita.

Fletcher brings size, speed, and a nasty edge that makes him dangerous in any kind of match. He is not in OWL to be compared to Will Ospreay, Aussie Open, or anyone from his past. He is here to prove the Protostar is not potential anymore. It is impact, ego, violence, and gold waiting to happen.

name: Calvin Tankman
whyImHere:
Calvin Tankman is proof that heavyweight wrestling does not have to move slow. Known as “Heavyweight Hustle” and “Indestructible,” Tankman brings the presence of a classic powerhouse with the surprise factor of someone who can strike fast, move quick, and hit offense most opponents never expect from a man his size. He has built his name across the independent scene, MLW, and DPW, where he became the inaugural DPW National Champion.

In OWL, Tankman is not here to be treated like a special attraction or a novelty big man. He is here to remind people that power, speed, and violence can live in the same body. Every opponent has a plan until Tankman starts walking them down. Then it becomes simple: survive the weight, survive the hands, survive the hustle.

name: Bandido
whyImHere:
Bandido arrives in OWL carrying the aura of a world-traveled luchador who has already proven he can thrive anywhere. AEW officially lists him as part of Brodido and as ROH World Champion, giving him the kind of championship credibility that instantly makes him more than just another masked high-flyer.

But Bandido’s danger is not only in the gold around his waist. It is in the blend: lucha speed, wild strength, fearless risk-taking, and the confidence of a man who has stood across from elite competition and never looked out of place. AEW itself has framed him as one of wrestling’s best, with a 2026 Revolution preview saying he made a case for being the best wrestler of 2025.

In OWL, Bandido is here to prove that honor, power, and spectacle can all wear the same mask.

name: Finn Balor
whyImHere:
Finn Bálor arrives in OWL as a man who has already lived several careers in one lifetime. Before WWE, before NXT, before becoming the first-ever Universal Champion, Bálor sharpened himself across Ireland and Japan, building the precision, discipline, and danger that made him impossible to ignore.

But Finn is not only technique and legacy. There is always something darker beneath the surface. Whether he walks in as the calm, calculating veteran or lets the Demon’s shadow creep closer, Bálor understands how to control a room before the fight even begins.

In OWL, he is not here to prove he belongs. That part is history. Finn Bálor is here to remind everyone that legends can still evolve—and still bite.

name: Yoshiki Inamura
whyImHere:
Yoshiki Inamura is not built like a visitor. He is built like someone OWL has to survive. A NOAH powerhouse with a background in judo and sumo, Inamura developed a reputation as a hard-hitting heavyweight prospect whose strength was sharpened through serious Strongman training.

After taking his fight overseas through Europe and WWE NXT, Inamura returned to Japan tougher, broader, and more dangerous, eventually capturing the GHC Heavyweight Championship and defending it through a hard-fought reign.

In OWL, Inamura brings the spirit of a classic Japanese monster ace: disciplined, physical, proud, and impossible to move once he plants his feet. He does not need flash to intimidate anyone. He lets the impact speak. Every strike, throw, and collision carries the same warning—Yoshiki Inamura came to test the foundation of this company.

name: Mike Bailey
whyImHere:
“Speedball” Mike Bailey arrives in OWL with a reputation built on movement, precision, and controlled violence. From Montreal to the international scene, Bailey became known for a striking style that feels almost impossible to time: lightning-fast kicks, sudden angles, and the kind of footwork that can make even elite opponents look a step behind. AEW lists him from Montreal, Quebec, Canada, and presents him under the “Speedball” identity.

But Bailey is more than flash. He has battled through major title scenes in AEW, including a World Championship challenge against Darby Allin and wars tied to JetSpeed’s rise and collapse. In OWL, Bailey is here to prove that speed is not a gimmick. It is pressure, timing, discipline, and impact delivered before the opponent can even react.

name: Ricky Sosa
whyImHere:
Ricky Sosa arrives in OWL with the kind of momentum that cannot be manufactured. Billed from Brussels, Belgium, Sosa turned heads as a European standout before becoming a viral sensation, powered by the crowd chanting “Bang, bang, Sosa” right along with him. TNA now lists him on its official roster, and his signing followed a breakout run that included his televised TNA debut and the buzz around his Blue Thunder Bang finisher.

But Sosa is not here just because the internet found him. He is here because the talent backs up the noise. Young, athletic, flashy, and fearless, Sosa wrestles like someone who knows the spotlight can disappear if you do not seize it. OWL is another stage, another crowd, and another chance to prove the chant was never hype—it was a warning.

name: Leon Slater
whyImHere:
Leon Slater does not wrestle like someone waiting for his time. He wrestles like someone who decided his time started already. Known as “The Youngest in Charge,” Slater came out of the UK scene with freakish athleticism, fearless confidence, and one of the most spectacular arsenals in wrestling. His rise in TNA made history when he became the youngest X-Division Champion ever at just 20 years old.

But Slater is more than a highlight reel. He has represented TNA during its crossover with WWE/NXT, defended the X-Division Title on NXT, and carried himself like a young star trusted with big moments before most wrestlers even find their footing. In OWL, Slater brings the Swanton 450, sky-high confidence, and the attitude of a man who is not chasing the future—he is already living in it.

name: Will Kroos
nickname: He's big, he's bad, he'll bodyslam your dad
whyImHere:
Mason Rook did not arrive in NXT quietly. Formerly known as Will Kroos on the British independent scene, Rook made his WWE presence felt by attacking NXT Champion Tony D’Angelo and immediately proving he was not built to wait in line. WWE presents him as a massive, dangerous force with surprising athletic ability, including one of the biggest moonsaults NXT has seen.

In OWL, Rook brings that same wrecking-ball energy: too big to ignore, too unpredictable to safely game-plan for, and too ambitious to be treated like just another newcomer. He has carried himself through PROGRESS, NOAH, and NXT like a man who knows his size is only part of the problem. Mason Rook is here to crush expectations, silence doubters, and prove that the new monster on the board moves however he wants.

name: Will Komplane
whyImHere:
Will Komplane believes wrestling has been ruined by everyone except him. As the self-appointed voice of The I.W.C., Komplane treats every match, promo, entrance, and mistake like evidence in a never-ending online argument. He does not just want to win—he wants to correct the entire industry.

Loud, smug, and impossible to satisfy, Will built The I.W.C. as a crusade against what he calls “bad booking, bad taste, and bad fans.” In reality, he is an arrogant control freak who thinks his opinions are facts and his complaints are wisdom. As the leader, Komplane does the talking, the judging, and the finger-pointing. OWL gives him a platform, and he plans to use it to shame the roster into his version of perfection.

name: Buzz Kihl
whyImHere:
Buzz Kihl is the reason people stop laughing when The I.W.C. walks into the room. While Will Komplane runs his mouth, Buzz handles the consequences. He is the muscle, the enforcer, and the blunt-force punctuation mark at the end of every complaint.

Buzz does not need long speeches or clever insults. He believes the best way to prove a point is to put someone through the mat and let Will explain why they deserved it afterward. As part of The I.W.C., Buzz acts like he is “cleaning up” OWL, but the truth is simpler: he likes hurting people under the cover of moral superiority. Komplane gives the orders, Buzz delivers the damage, and together they plan to censor anyone who does not fit their standards.

name: Levi Martín
whyImHere:
Levi Martin grew up with every advantage money could buy, but comfort bored him. He did not need wrestling to survive—he chose it because he wanted something that could finally push back. That search led him to Syrup City Wrestling Academy, where privilege met punishment and Levi learned that talent means nothing if you cannot take a hit.

At SCWA, he found Connor Canada, and together they became True North Elite: two Canadians with different backgrounds but the same hunger for control, respect, and championship gold. Levi brings the polish, arrogance, and cold confidence of a man used to getting what he wants. He is not here to prove he belongs with the common man. He is here to prove he was born above them.

name: Connor Canada
whyImHere:
Connor Canada was supposed to be a great hockey goalie. He had the talent, the reflexes, and the toughness—but he also had a problem: he could not stop fighting people. On the ice, off the ice, after whistles, before whistles, Connor always found a reason to drop the gloves. When your goalie is skating across the rink looking for smoke, something has gone very wrong.

Syrup City Wrestling Academy gave him a place to turn that anger into something useful. There, he met Levi Martin, and the two became True North Elite, tearing through the Canadian indies with a mix of privilege, violence, and championship ambition. Connor is the mean streak of the team: rough, angry, physical, and proud of it. OWL is just the next arena, and he still has no interest in staying in his crease.

name: Ricochet
whyImHere:
Ricochet comes to OWL with a reputation that speaks before he ever leaves his feet. AEW lists him as part of The Demand, and that name fits the version of Ricochet standing in front of the world now: sharper, colder, and no longer interested in being admired only for highlights. He wants control, attention, and proof that the same people who praised his athleticism should have feared his ambition too.

He has been called one of the most spectacular athletes in wrestling for a reason, but Ricochet is not here to be anyone’s human GIF. He is a former inaugural AEW National Champion, winning the title in a Casino Gauntlet at Full Gear, and he brings that same opportunistic edge to OWL. Every flip, strike, and sudden counter is no longer just beautiful—it is a warning. Ricochet is here to remind everyone that gravity was never his limit. It was everyone else’s.

name: Johnny Gargano
whyImHere:
Johnny Gargano has spent his career proving that being smaller never meant being lesser. Built on heart, precision, and the refusal to stay down, Gargano came to OWL looking for a new mountain to climb. But this time, he did not want just another singles chase. He wanted a partner who could match his obsession for wrestling at its purest level.

That led him to Zack Sabre Jr. At first, they did not like each other much. Johnny saw Zack as cold and arrogant. Zack saw Johnny as emotional and dramatic. Then they got in the ring together and realized the same thing: nobody else thinks this deeply about wrestling. Gargano brings the heart, pace, and resilience. Zack brings the cruelty, control, and technique. Together, they are chasing one goal: to prove great wrestling still wins.
team: B.T.T.I.T.W (Best Tag Team In The World)

name: Zack Sabre Jr.
hometown: The Isle of Sheppey in Kent, England
whyImHere:
Zack Sabre Jr. does not believe in wasted motion, wasted emotion, or wasted opponents. He sees wrestling as a problem to be solved, a body to be dismantled, and a mind to be embarrassed before the bell ever stops ringing. When he came to OWL, he had no interest in friendship, nostalgia, or playing nice with the roster.

Then Johnny Gargano became useful.

Zack respected Johnny’s toughness before he respected the man himself. Gargano kept surviving positions most wrestlers would panic in, kept adapting, and kept forcing Zack to think one step further. That interested him. Zack still thinks Johnny is too sentimental, too dramatic, and entirely too American about things—but he also knows Gargano can fight, endure, and create openings. For Zack, that makes him a rare kind of partner: one worth tolerating.
team: B.T.T.I.T.W (Best Tag Team In The World)

name: Brody King
whyImHere: Brody King brings chaos with him everywhere he goes. Massive, brutal, and completely unbothered by pain, he fights like a man who enjoys turning matches into disasters. There’s an aura around Brody that makes opponents uneasy before the bell even rings, and once it does, that feeling usually gets worse. He doesn’t just want to beat people — he wants to overwhelm them, break their spirit, and leave a lasting impression. As one half of Black Tide, Brody is the storm itself: violent, suffocating, and impossible to stop once he gets rolling. OWL isn’t ready for the damage he plans to do.

name: Jeff Cobb
whyImHere: Jeff Cobb didn’t come to OWL to fit in — he came to run through people. A world-class powerhouse with freak athleticism, Cobb blends raw island strength with explosive speed that few men his size should even have. He built his name by ragdolling opponents, throwing them across the ring, and making violence look effortless. But on his own, Cobb is dangerous. Aligned with Brody King, he becomes something worse. In Black Tide, Cobb is the engine of destruction: a relentless force who crushes resistance and drowns anyone standing in the way. He’s here for domination, plain and simple.

name: Gabe Kidd
whyImHere:
Gabe Kidd does not wrestle like a man looking for respect. He wrestles like someone looking for a receipt. Loud, violent, reckless, and proud of every scar he leaves behind, Kidd came to OWL with the kind of reputation that makes promoters nervous and opponents check the exit before the bell rings.

Joining Bounty Hunnaz gave him something he always understood: a target. Gabe does not need a championship speech or some grand mission. Point him at somebody, give him a reason, and he will turn the match into a crime scene. He is not here to play wrestler. He is here to collect debts, break mouths, and make sure every team in OWL learns that running from Gabe Kidd only makes the hunt more fun.

name: Jacob Fatu
whyImHere:
Jacob Fatu is not the kind of man you hire unless the situation has already gotten ugly. A destroyer with terrifying speed, violent instinct, and bloodline power behind every strike, Fatu does not just beat opponents—he overwhelms them. He is the kind of force that makes a team feel outnumbered even when the numbers are even.

In Bounty Hunnaz, Jacob is the nightmare at the end of the chase. Gabe Kidd brings the chaos, but Fatu brings the final consequence. Once he locks onto a target, there is no escape route, no safe corner, and no clever plan that survives contact. OWL did not bring him in for peace. It brought him in because some people only understand fear when it has a face.

name: Rush
whyImHere:
RUSH does not enter OWL looking for permission, approval, or friendship. El Toro Blanco walks in like every room already belongs to him, and if anyone disagrees, he is more than happy to make the conversation physical. Violent, proud, and impossible to intimidate, RUSH brings the kind of pressure that makes opponents feel trapped before the bell even rings.

In Los Ingobernables: Plata o Plomo, RUSH is the force that sets the tone. He does not negotiate softly. He does not chase respect. He takes it, breaks it, and leaves the rest of the roster to decide whether they want to pay tribute or pay the price. OWL wanted stars with presence. RUSH brings conquest.

name: Andrade
whyImHere:
Andrade enters OWL with elegance, arrogance, and danger wrapped into one man. He is too polished to look desperate, too talented to be ignored, and too proud to settle for being treated like anyone else. Every movement carries purpose, from the way he walks to the way he punishes an opponent for one mistake.

As part of Los Ingobernables: Plata o Plomo, Andrade is the cold hand behind the threat. Where RUSH brings the storm, Andrade brings calculation. He knows when to strike, when to manipulate, and when to let someone think they are safe right before taking everything from them. OWL is not just another stop for Andrade. It is another empire waiting to be dressed in gold.

name: Dragon Lee
whyImHere:
Dragon Lee has built his name on speed, precision, and fearless lucha excellence, but Double Dragon gives him something different: family at his side. He has never needed anyone to prove how dangerous he is, but teaming with Dralístico turns his confidence into something sharper. This is not just a reunion of brothers. It is a statement that bloodline, pride, and lucha tradition still matter in a tag division full of chaos.

Dragon Lee brings the cleaner edge of the team: fast combinations, explosive counters, and the discipline to strike at exactly the right second. In OWL, he is not here to live off reputation. He is here to show that even a dragon is more dangerous when it is not flying alone.

name: Dralistico
whyImHere:
Dralístico walks into OWL with the swagger of a man who knows exactly where he comes from and exactly what his family name means. Flashy, dangerous, and impossible to fully control, he brings the wild side of lucha libre to Double Dragon. Where Dragon Lee attacks with precision, Dralístico attacks with flair, risk, and attitude.

This team gives him more than a partner. It gives him a stage to prove that he is not standing in anyone’s shadow—not even his brother’s. Dralístico is here to turn every match into a spectacle, every mistake into an opening, and every opponent into another reminder that family pride can be violent when tested. In OWL, he is not just teaming with Dragon Lee. He is helping unleash both dragons at once.

name: Sheamus O'Shaunessy
whyImHere:
Sheamus has never needed luck, but he has always understood a fight. Built on barroom toughness, veteran instincts, and a career full of championship scars, Sheamus came to OWL looking for the kind of violence that keeps a man sharp. He has done the big moments, the bright lights, and the title runs. Now he wants something meaner: a tag division that can actually test him.

That is where Jordan Devlin comes in. Sheamus saw the same Irish edge in him — pride, anger, skill, and just enough spite to make people uncomfortable. Together, Hard Luck is not about friendship or nostalgia. It is about punishment. Sheamus brings the heavy hands, the veteran cruelty, and the reminder that if you step across from him, you better be ready for a proper scrap.

name: Jordan Devlin
whyImHere:
Jordan Devlin has spent too long being overlooked, renamed, underestimated, or treated like somebody else’s side story. In OWL, he gets to be exactly what he is: a dangerous Irish technician with a chip on his shoulder and no interest in playing nice. Devlin is sharp, bitter, talented, and always one clean opening away from making someone regret dismissing him.

Teaming with Sheamus gives him muscle, credibility, and a partner who understands that Irish pride is not just something you wave around — it is something you swing like a weapon. Devlin brings the precision to Hard Luck, picking opponents apart while Sheamus breaks them down. He is not here to be carried by a legend. He is here to prove he belongs beside one.

name: Rob Van Dam
whyImHere:
Rob Van Dam has never needed to explain himself. He walks into OWL with the same calm confidence that made him one of wrestling’s most original stars: relaxed, dangerous, and always one kick away from changing the whole match. RVD does not chase trends, and he definitely does not force chemistry. He just vibes, fights, flies, and lets the crowd catch up.

That is why Matt Riddle somehow makes perfect sense. RVD sees the same laid-back confidence in him, but with a different kind of edge. Where Riddle brings barefoot combat and MMA pressure, RVD brings veteran timing, educated feet, and highlight-reel creativity. Together, Dude…Bro is not trying to be normal. They are trying to be smooth, unpredictable, and impossible to prepare for.

name: Matt Riddle
whyImHere:
Matt Riddle came to OWL with bare feet, big confidence, and no interest in overthinking anything. He fights like a man who can smile through a conversation and then suddenly fold someone in half. That easygoing attitude makes people underestimate him, but Riddle’s background makes the truth pretty simple: relaxed does not mean harmless.

Teaming with Rob Van Dam gives Riddle the perfect partner. RVD understands rhythm, timing, and controlled chaos better than almost anyone. Riddle brings grappling, strikes, and that “bro, I got this” energy that either drives opponents crazy or gets them knocked out. Dude…Bro works because neither man is pretending. They are chill, confident, dangerous, and fully capable of winning a match before anyone realizes things got serious.
